title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police seek suspect in Dorchester armed robbery involving multiple victims on Jan. 22 near Adams and Whitten Streets.
The suspect reportedly approached victims from behind and robbed them at gunpoint around 4:30 p.m. on Jan. 22.
Police request public help identifying the suspect and urge tips via 617-343-4335 or anonymously through 1-800-494-TIPS.

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough may deny World Cup license if $7.8 million in public safety funding isn't provided upfront.
FIFA's Gillette Stadium host committee promises payment within two days post-games, but Foxborough demands advance funding.
Foxborough's concerns persist despite assurances from Boston Soccer 2026 and Krafts to cover funding shortfalls.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu will host 'office hours' Thursday for residents to discuss city issues directly with her.
The event at Bruce C. Bolling Building in Roxbury will feature department staff from over a dozen city offices.
Services offered include voting registration, handicap parking applications, and birth, marriage, and death certificate printings.

title: 7 players Bruins could target at 2026 NHL trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins considering trade options before Friday's deadline, focusing on long-term players over rentals.
Potential targets include Robert Thomas and Simon Nemec, both requiring significant assets to acquire.
Defense upgrades eyed with players like Rasmus Ristolainen and Justin Faulk as possible additions.

title: Staff member at special needs residential program in Franklin accused of breaking autistic student’s arm
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Saturs Allison, 27, of Pawtucket, R.I., was indicted for allegedly breaking an autistic student's arm at a Franklin group home.
The incident was caught on security footage; Allison allegedly used excessive force against the student, violating program protocols.
Allison has been charged with assault and battery on a disabled person causing serious injury and faces up to five years in prison.

title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Lewis, 18, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her friend Lily, who died from brain cancer.
Lewis is participating in the marathon for the Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge alongside her father.
Her goal is to raise funds for cancer research, supporting those affected by cancer through her runs.

title: Fall River man sentenced for raping woman when she refused to take naked pictures of young daughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Elias Rodrigues Dos Santos Mendes, 55, pleaded guilty to rape and child pornography charges in Fall River Superior Court.
He was sentenced to eight to ten years in prison and will be deported to Brazil after serving his sentence.
The charges followed a March 2024 incident where he raped a woman who refused to take nude photos of her child.

title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots plan to release Stefon Diggs, freeing up $17 million in cap space.
New England targets WR Alec Pierce or A.J. Brown to fill Diggs' spot.
Patriots consider trading or drafting new wide receivers to bolster roster.

title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brianna Poehler is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Miles for Miracles, supporting Boston Children's Hospital.
Her daughter, a liver transplant survivor, received life-saving care at Boston Children's Hospital at 11 months old.
Poehler aims to raise funds for research, treatments, and care for children facing similar health challenges.

title: Do you support the U.S. strikes on Iran? Here’s what 300 readers said.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: 54% of 350 respondents support US strikes on Iran, with 43% opposed and 3% unsure.
Pro-strike readers cite Iran's role in regional conflicts and describe the government as a 'brutal regime.'
Opponents fear escalating conflict and unauthorized action without congressional approval, stressing focus on domestic priorities.
